verses.text: Ten curtains of fine twined linen, blue, purple, scarlet, with cherubim of cunning work — each twenty-eight by four cubits. Five coupled together, five coupled. Fifty loops of blue on each set; fifty gold taches couple them — that the tabernacle may be one. Guidance: The innermost layer Israel would see — woven cherubim watching from above. Two sets joined by gold into one. God's house is made one by what crosses the seam.

Make eleven curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle — thirty by four cubits each. Couple five and six. Fifty loops, fifty brass taches, couple the tent as one. Rams' skins dyed red, then badgers' skins above. Guidance: Four layers cover the holy place — linen, goats' hair, rams' skins, badgers' skins. From inside it's woven art; from outside, plain leather. God's beauty is hidden under His ordinariness.

Boards of shittim wood standing up, ten cubits long, one and a half wide, two tenons each. Twenty south, forty silver sockets; twenty north, forty sockets. Six west, two corners coupled at the head with one ring. Sixteen sockets beneath. Guidance: Forty-eight boards stand like a forest plated in gold, each rooted in silver. Beauty rests on what is buried — every standing thing in God's house has been set down before.

Make bars of shittim wood — five for each side, five for the rear; the middle bar shoots through from end to end. Overlay boards with gold; rings of gold for the bars; overlay the bars. Set up the tabernacle after the pattern shewn thee. Guidance: One bar runs the whole length, threading every board into a single body. Holiness is held together by what runs hidden through the middle.

Make a vail of blue, purple, scarlet, fine linen with cherubim — on four pillars of shittim, gold hooks in silver sockets. Hang it under the taches; bring the ark within. The vail divides the holy from the most holy. Set the mercy seat on the ark; the table outside, candlestick opposite. Guidance: One vail with cherubim hides the throne. Hebrews says Christ's body was this vail — torn the day He died, opening the holy of holies.

Make a hanging for the door of the tent — blue, purple, scarlet, fine twined linen, of needlework. Make five pillars of shittim wood, overlaid with gold; their hooks of gold; cast five sockets of brass for them. Guidance: The outer door is needlework, not cunning work — the artistry steps down as you move from God's inmost room outward. Even the threshold of His tent is dignified.
